mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.2 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and decrease in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a positive supply shock with stable pricing, creating favorable conditions for equity market gains over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO*CPIAUCSL) fails to explain more than 5% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or the directional relationship reverses in 30%+ of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly acceleration in GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% signals robust economic demand that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high-GDP growth periods fall below baseline returns in 50%+ of cases, or the predictive power of GDPC1 drops below 0.15 in rolling 5-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average indicates weakening manufacturing activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative relationship between INDPRO declines and SPX returns breaks down in 40%+ of cases, or the 5-day return volatility in triggered periods exceeds 2.5% without corresponding SPX declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% signals central bank policy tightening, creating headwinds for equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high-inflation periods show positive correlation with CPIAUCSL, or the predictive accuracy of the signal falls below 55% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decrease in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a strong labor market that boosts consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS*UNRATE) explains less than 3% of SPX return variance, or the directional relationship reverses in 35%+ of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,000,000 units signals weak demand for construction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during housing market downturns.
failure_condition: SPX returns in low-housing-start periods show positive correlation with HOUST, or the signal's predictive power drops below 0.10 in rolling 5-year tests.
